title: eulophia rugulosa
intro: Eulophia rugulosa is a species of orchid found in tropical Africa, commonly known as the "saxophone orchid" due to its unique, curved flower shape. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Eulophia rugulosa include the treatment of fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, while also having potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tea or infusion, or used in traditional remedies, such as poultices or ointments, to harness its medicinal properties.
